experiment: v0.4.3 compact Books P1 A/B test

A.name: Variant A — Basic Grounding
A.grounding_policy: Metadata is context only — do NOT restate the genre, category, or title as intent.
A.metadata_dominant_rule: Use sparingly; prefer review_plus_metadata whenever any review signal exists.
A.key_difference: Permits review_plus_metadata when metadata helps clarify genre context.

B.name: Variant B — Stronger Grounding
B.grounding_policy: Metadata is disambiguation context only, NOT a source for constructing intent.
B.metadata_dominant_rule: Explicit: if review is generic and metadata is needed, set is_discriminative=false AND grounding_level=metadata_dominant.
B.key_difference: Forbids generating intent from metadata; metadata_dominant = rejection signal.

winner_rationale: Variant B achieves 10% higher eligible_rate (0.97 vs 0.87). The review_plus_metadata=8 cases in A indicate the model used metadata to supplement weak review signals — B's stricter policy correctly marks these as ineligible or extracts from review alone. Leakage difference (8 vs 10) is negligible false-positive genre words. Latency difference is negligible.

pilot_results.leakage_note: Confirmed false positives — genre words appearing in both title and semantically-appropriate source_text. Not title copying.
